name: payu

metadata.name: payu
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: A climate model workflow manager for supercomputing environments

documentation:
# Payu

```{image} https://github.com/payu-org/payu/actions/workflows/CI.yml/badge.svg
:target: https://github.com/payu-org/payu/actions/workflows/CI.yml
```

```{image} https://github.com/payu-org/payu/actions/workflows/CD.yml/badge.svg
:target: https://github.com/payu-org/payu/actions/workflows/CD.yml
```

```{image} https://coveralls.io/repos/github/payu-org/payu/badge.svg?branch=master&killcache=1
:target: https://coveralls.io/github/payu-org/payu?branch=master
```

```{image} https://readthedocs.org/projects/docs/badge/?version=latest
:target: https://payu.readthedocs.io/en/latest/
```

Payu is a climate model workflow management tool for supercomputing
environments.

Payu is currently only configured for use on computing clusters maintained by
NCI (National Computational Infrastructure) in Australia.

See the [documentation] for more details.

[documentation]: http://payu.readthedocs.org/en/latest/

documentation: Read the existing configuration log.

documentation: Prepare the stages in the CABLE configuration.

methods.name: payu.models.staged_cable.StagedCable._get_further_restarts
documentation: Get the restarts from stages further in the past where necessary.

methods.name: payu.models.staged_cable.StagedCable._get_stage_name
documentation: Get the name of the stage being prepared.

methods.name: payu.models.staged_cable.StagedCable._apply_stage_namelists
documentation:
Apply the stage namelists to the master namelists.

The master namelist is the namelist that exists in the control
directory and the stage namelist exists within the directory for the
given stage. If a master version of a given namelist does not exist,
then the stage namelist is taken as is.

Example:
.
├── cable.nml
└── cable_stage
    ├── cable.nml
    └── luc.nml

In this instance, the ```cable.nml``` for ```cable_stage``` would be
a merge of the top level ```cable.nml``` and
```cable_stage/cable.nml``` (with the latter taking precedence) and
```luc.nml``` is just ```cable_stage/luc.nml```.

methods.name: payu.models.staged_cable.StagedCable._handle_configuration_log_setup
documentation:
Make appropriate adjustments to the configuration log to reflect
that the setup of the stage is complete.

methods.name: payu.models.staged_cable.StagedCable.archive
documentation:
Store model output to laboratory archive and update the
configuration log.

methods.name: payu.models.staged_cable.StagedCable._save_configuration_log
documentation: Write the updated configuration log back to the staging area.
A driver for running staged CABLE spin-up configurations.

documentation: Construct the list of files to be tracked by the runlog.

methods.name: payu.runlog.Runlog.push
documentation:
Push the changes to the remote repository.

Usage: payu push

This command pushes local runlog changes to the remote runlog
repository, currently named `payu`, using the SSH key associated with
this experiment.

For an experiment `test`, it is equivalent to the following command:

```
ssh-agent bash -c "
    ssh-add $HOME/.ssh/payu/id_rsa_payu_test
    git push --all payu
"
```

methods.name: payu.runlog.Runlog.github_setup
documentation: Set up authentication keys and API tokens.

functions.name: payu.models.cable._get_forcing_path
documentation:
Return the met forcing file path for a given variable and year.

Parameters
----------
variable : str
    Variable name.
year : int
    Year value.
input_dir : str
    Path to work input directory.
offset : list of int, optional
    Offset the current simulation year from `offset[0]` to `offset[1]`
    before inferring the met forcing path.
repeat : list of int, optional
    Constrain the current simulation year between `repeat[0]` and
    `repeat[1]` (inclusive) before inferring the met forcing path. If the
    year is outside the interval, the constrained year repeats over the
    interval.

Returns
-------
path : str
    Path (relative to control directory) to the inferred met forcing file.

Raises
------
FileNotFoundError
    If unable to infer met forcing path.

functions.name: payu.models.cice.read_binary_iced_header
documentation: Read header information from a CICE4 binary restart file.

methods.name: payu.models.cice.Cice._calc_runtime
documentation:
Calculate 1: the previous number of timesteps simulated, and 2:
the number of timesteps to simulate in the next run.
Modifies the working self.ice_in namelist in place

Note 1: This method is overridden in the cice5 driver.

Note 2: For ESM1.5, the actual model start date and run time are
controlled via the separate input_ice.nml namelist, with relevant
calculations in the access driver.

methods.name: payu.models.cice.Cice.get_log_files
documentation:
Find model log files in the work directory based on regex patterns
in self.logs_to_compress.

Returns
-------
log_files: list of paths to model log files.

methods.name: payu.models.cice.Cice.compress_log_files
documentation: Compress model log files into tarball.

methods.name: payu.models.cice.Cice._make_restart_ptr
documentation:
CICE4 restart pointers are created in the access driver, where
the correct run start dates are available.

methods.name: payu.models.cice.Cice.overwrite_restart_ptr
documentation:
Generate restart pointer file 'ice.restart_file' pointing to
'iced.YYYYMMDD' with the correct start date.
Additionally check that the `iced.YYYYMNDD` restart file's header
has the correct previous runtime.
Typically called from the access driver, which provides the
the correct date and runtime.

Parameters
----------
run_start_date: datetime.date
    Start date of the new simulation
previous_runtime:  int
    Seconds between experiment initialisation date and start date
calendar_file:  str
    Calendar restart file used to calculate timing information

methods.name: payu.models.cice.Cice._cice4_check_date_consistency
documentation:
Check that the previous runtime in iced restart file header
matches the runtime calculated from the calendar restart file.

Parameters
----------
iced_path: str or Path
    Path to iced restart file
previous_runtime:  int
    Seconds between experiment initialisation date and start date
calendar_file:  str or Path
    Calendar restart file used to calculate timing information

methods.name: payu.models.cice.Cice.find_matching_iced
documentation:
Check a directory for an iced.YYYYMMDD restart file matching a
specified date.
Raises an error if the expected file is not found.

Parameters
----------
dir_path: str or Path
    Path to directory containing iced restart files
date: datetime.date
    Date for matching iced file names

Returns
-------
iced_file_name: str
    Name of iced restart file found in dir_path matching
    the specified date

functions.name: payu.calendar.seconds_between_dates
documentation:
Calculate the number of seconds between two datetime objects
with a specified calender type by using cftime datetime objects
as intermiaries.

Parameters
----------
start_date: datetime.date
end_date: datetime.date
caltype: Integer, either GREGORIAN or NOLEAP

Returns
-------
seconds: Number of seconds between start_date and end_date.
TODO: The caltype logic could be simplified if we switched
to using just a string as the caltype input. Might require reworking of other
functions for consistency.

functions.name: payu.calendar.date_to_cftime
documentation:
Convert a datetime.datetime object to a cftime.datetime object which
has the same year, month, day, hour, minute, second values.

Parameters
----------
date: datetime.date object
calendar: string specifying a valid cftime calendar type

Returns
-------
date_cf: cftime.datetime object.

functions.name: payu.calendar.add_year_start_offset_to_datetime
documentation:
Return a cftime datetime at the start of the year, that is n years

functions.name: payu.calendar.add_month_start_offset_to_datetime
documentation:
Return a cftime datetime of the start of the month, that is n months

functions.name: payu.calendar.add_timedelta_fn
documentation:
Returns a function that takes initial datetime and multiplier n,
and returns a datetime that is n * offset from the initial datetime

functions.name: payu.calendar.parse_date_offset
documentation: Parse a given string date offset string and return an DatetimeOffset

classes.name: payu.calendar.DatetimeOffset

methods.name: payu.calendar.DatetimeOffset.add_to_datetime
documentation:
Takes an initial cftime datetime,
and returns a datetime with the offset added

A utility class for adding various time offsets to cftime datetimes.

Parameters:
    unit (str): The unit of the time offset. Supported units are:
        - "YS" for years (start of the year)
        - "MS" for months (start of the month)
        - "W" for weeks
        - "D" for days
        - "H" for hours
        - "T" for minutes
        - "S" for seconds
    magnitude (int): The magnitude of the time offset.

Methods:
    - `add_to_datetime(initial_dt: cftime.datetime) -> cftime.datetime`:
      Adds the specified time offset to the given cftime datetime and
      returns the resulting datetime.

Attributes:
    - unit (str): The unit of the time offset.
    - magnitude (int): The magnitude of the time offset.

documentation:
Turn a mount point point into the keyword used to specify storages,

functions.name: payu.schedulers.pbs.find_mounts
documentation:
Search a path for a matching mount point and return a set of unique
NCI compatible strings to add to the qsub command

Return a list of paths from manifest files to use to check for
storage paths

modules.name: payu.metadata
documentation:
Payu experiment UUID and metadata support

Generates and commit a new experiment uuid and updates/creates experiment
metadata

:copyright: Copyright 2011 Marshall Ward, see AUTHORS for details.
:license: Apache License, Version 2.0, see LICENSE for details.

functions.name: payu.metadata.get_schema_from_github
documentation: Retrieve metadata schema from github

functions.name: payu.metadata.add_template_metadata_values
documentation: Add in templates for un-set metadata values

functions.name: payu.metadata.generate_uuid
documentation: Generate a new uuid

classes.name: payu.metadata.Metadata

methods.name: payu.metadata.Metadata.read_file
documentation: Read metadata file - preserving orginal format if it exists

methods.name: payu.metadata.Metadata.setup
documentation:
Set UUID and experiment name.

Parameters:
    keep_uuid: bool, default False
        Keep pre-existing UUID, if it exists.
    is_new_experiment: bool, default False
        If not keep_uuid, generate a new UUID and a branch-uuid aware
        experiment name. This is set in payu.branch.checkout_branch.
Return: None

Note: Experiment name is the name used for the work and archive
directories in the Laboratory.

methods.name: payu.metadata.Metadata.new_experiment_name
documentation: Generate a new experiment name

methods.name: payu.metadata.Metadata.set_experiment_name
documentation:
Set experiment name - this is used for work and archive
sub-directories in the Laboratory

methods.name: payu.metadata.Metadata.has_archive
documentation:
Return True if archive under the experiment name exists and
if it exists, check for a non-matching UUID in archive metadata.

methods.name: payu.metadata.Metadata.set_new_uuid
documentation: Generate a new uuid and set experiment name

methods.name: payu.metadata.Metadata.write_metadata
documentation:
Create/update metadata file, commit any changes and
copy metadata file to the experiment archive.

Parameters:
    restart_path: Optional[Path]
        Prior restart path - used for finding parent metadata
    set_template_values: bool, default False
        Read schema and set metadata template values for new
        experiments
    parent_experiment: Optional[str]
        Parent experiment UUID to add to generated metadata

Return: None

Note: This assumes setup() has been run to set UUID and experiment name

methods.name: payu.metadata.Metadata.update_file
documentation: Write any updates to metadata file

methods.name: payu.metadata.Metadata.get_model_name
documentation: Get model name from config file

methods.name: payu.metadata.Metadata.get_parent_experiment
documentation:
Searches UUID in the metadata in the parent directory that
contains the restart

methods.name: payu.metadata.Metadata.commit_file
documentation:
Add a git commit for changes to metadata file, if file has changed
and if control path is a git repository

methods.name: payu.metadata.Metadata.copy_to_archive
documentation: Copy metadata file to archive

Class to store/update/create metadata such as experiment uuid and name

Parameters:
    laboratory_archive_path : Path
        The archive sub-directory in Laboratory
    branch : Optional[str]
        The git branch on which the experiment is run
    control_path : Optional[Path]
        Path to where the experiment is configured and run. The default
        is set to the current working directory. This default is set in
        in fsops.read_config
    config_path : Optional[Path]
        Configuration Path. The default is config.yaml in the current
        working directory. This is also set in fsop.read_config
    disabled : bool, default False
        Flag to disable metadata and UUID generation and commits. The
        legacy name (control directory name) for experiments names
        in archive will be used instead.

modules.name: payu.namcouple
documentation:
payu.namcouple
==============

OASIS Namcouple parser

:copyright: Copyright 2014 Nicholas Hannah

classes.name: payu.namcouple.Namcouple

methods.name: payu.namcouple.Namcouple.substitute_timestep
documentation: Substitute a new timestep value using regex.

Class to represent an OASIS namcouple file.

Allows fields to be modified.

Presently only supports $RUNTIME and ocean timestep.

modules.name: payu.manifest
documentation:
payu.manifest
===============

Provides an manifest class to store manifest data, which uses a
subclassed yamanifest PayuManifest class

:copyright: Copyright 2019 Aidan Heerdegen, see AUTHORS for details.
:license: Apache License, Version 2.0, see LICENSE for details.

classes.name: payu.manifest.PayuManifest

methods.name: payu.manifest.PayuManifest.calculate_fast
documentation:
Calculate hash value for all filepaths using a fast hash function and
fall back to slower full hash functions if fast hashes fail to agree,
with the pre-existing manifest

methods.name: payu.manifest.PayuManifest.check_reproduce
documentation: Compare full hashes with previous manifest

methods.name: payu.manifest.PayuManifest.add_filepath
documentation:
Bespoke function to add filepath & fullpath to manifest
object without hashing. Can defer hashing until all files are
added. Hashing all at once is much faster as overhead for
threading is spread over all files

methods.name: payu.manifest.PayuManifest.add_fast
documentation:
Bespoke function to add filepaths but set shortcircuit to True, which
means only the first calculable hash will be stored. In this way only
one "fast" hashing function need be called for each filepath.

methods.name: payu.manifest.PayuManifest.copy_file
documentation: Returns flag which says to copy rather than link a file.

methods.name: payu.manifest.PayuManifest.make_link
documentation:
Payu integration function for creating symlinks in work directories
which point back to the original file.

methods.name: payu.manifest.PayuManifest.copy
documentation: Copy myself to another location

A manifest object sub-classed from yamanifest object with some payu
specific additions and enhancements.

classes.name: payu.manifest.Manifest

methods.name: payu.manifest.Manifest.__iter__
documentation: Iterator method

methods.name: payu.manifest.Manifest.__len__
documentation: Return the number of manifests in the manifest class.

methods.name: payu.manifest.Manifest.load_manifests
documentation: Load pre-existing manifests

methods.name: payu.manifest.Manifest.add_filepath
documentation:
Wrapper to the add_filepath function in PayuManifest. Prevents outside
code from directly calling anything in PayuManifest.

methods.name: payu.manifest.Manifest.get_all_previous_fullpaths
documentation: Return a list of all fullpaths in manifest files

A Manifest class which stores all manifests for file tracking and
methods to operate on them

documentation:
Run the modulecmd tool and use its Python-formatted output to set the

Run module use + load commands for user-defined modules. Return
tuple containing a set of loaded modules and paths added to
LOADEDMODULES and PATH environment variable, as result of

modules.name: payu.backports
documentation:
Backport of subprocess.check_output for python 2.6.

Thanks to Eduardo Felipe (@edufelipe at GitHub)

https://gist.github.com/edufelipe/1027906

functions.name: payu.backports.check_output
documentation:
Run command with arguments and return its output as a byte string.
Backported from Python 2.7 as it's implemented as pure python on stdlib.
\>>> check_output(['/usr/bin/python', '--version'])
Python 2.6.2

modules.name: payu.branch
documentation:
Experiment branch support for payu's branch, clone and checkout commands

This may generate new experiment ID, updates, sets any
specified configuration in config.yaml and updates work/archive symlinks

:copyright: Copyright 2011 Marshall Ward, see AUTHORS for details.
:license: Apache License, Version 2.0, see LICENSE for details.

functions.name: payu.branch.check_restart
documentation:
Checks if restart path exists and whether the archive already
has pre-existing restarts. Returns a resolved restart path

Parameters
----------
restart_path: Path
    Absolute, or relative, restart path to start experiment from
archive_path: Optional[Path], default None
    Experiment archive directory to check for pre-existing restarts files

Returns
----------
Optional[Path]
    Absolute restart path if a valid path, otherwise None

functions.name: payu.branch.add_restart_to_config
documentation:
Takes restart path and config path, and add 'restart' flag to the
config file - which is used to start a run if there isn't a pre-existing
restart in archive

functions.name: payu.branch.get_control_path
documentation: Given the config path, return the control path

functions.name: payu.branch.check_config_path
documentation: Checks if configuration file exists

functions.name: payu.branch.checkout_branch
documentation:
Checkout branch, setup metadata and add symlinks

Parameters
----------
branch_name : str
    Name of branch to checkout/create
is_new_branch: bool, default False
    Create new branch and mark as new experiment
is_new_experiment: bool, default False
    Create new uuid for this experiment
keep_uuid: bool, default False
    Keep UUID unchanged, if it exists - this overrides is_new_experiment
    if there is a pre-existing UUID
start_point: Optional[str]
    Branch name or commit hash to start new branch from
restart_path: Optional[Path]
    Restart path to start experiment from
config_path: Optional[Path]
    Path to configuration file - config.yaml
control_path: Optional[Path]
    Path to control directory - defaults to current working directory
model_type: Optional[str]
    Type of model - used for creating a Laboratory
lab_path: Optional[Path]
    Path to laboratory directory
parent_experiment: Optional[str]
    Parent experiment UUID to add to generated metadata

functions.name: payu.branch.switch_symlink
documentation:
Helper function for removing and switching work and archive
symlinks in control directory

functions.name: payu.branch.clone
documentation:
Clone an experiment control repository.

Parameters:
    repository: str
        Git URL or path to Git repository to clone
    directory: Path
        The control directory where the repository will be cloned
    branch: Optional[str]
        Name of branch to checkout during the git clone
    start_point: Optional[str]
        Branch-name/commit/tag to start new branch from
    new_branch_name: Optional[str]
        Name of new branch to create and checkout
    keep_uuid: bool, default False
        Keep UUID unchanged, if it exists
    config_path: Optional[Path]
        Path to configuration file - config.yaml
    control_path: Optional[Path]
        Path to control directory - defaults to current working directory
    model_type: Optional[str]
        Type of model - used for creating a Laboratory
    lab_path: Optional[Path]
        Path to laboratory directory
    restart_path: Optional[Path]
        Restart path to start experiment from
    parent_experiment: Optional[str]
        Parent experiment UUID to add to generated metadata

Returns: None

Note: branch, if defined, can be set to avoid initially checking out the
default branch during git clone - this is useful for repositories where
the default branch is not a payu configuration.

functions.name: payu.branch.get_branch_metadata
documentation: Return dictionary of branch metadata if it exists, None otherwise

functions.name: payu.branch.contains_config
documentation: Checks if config file in defined in given branch

functions.name: payu.branch.print_branch_metadata
documentation:
Display given Git branch UUID, or if config.yaml or metadata.yaml does
not exist.

Parameters:
    branch: git.Head
        Branch object to parse commit tree.
    verbose: bool, default False
        Display entire metadata files
    remote: bool, default False
        Display remote Git branches

Returns: None

functions.name: payu.branch.list_branches
documentation:
Display local Git branches UUIDs.

Parameters:
    verbose: bool, default False
        Display entire metadata files
    remote: bool, default False
        Display remote Git branches

Returns: None

modules.name: payu.sync
documentation:
Experiment post-processing - syncing archive to a remote directory

:copyright: Copyright 2011 Marshall Ward, see AUTHORS for details.
:license: Apache License, Version 2.0, see LICENSE for details.

classes.name: payu.sync.SourcePath
documentation:
Helper class for building rsync commands - stores attributes
of source paths to sync.
Note: Protected paths are paths that shouldn't be removed
locally if still running an experiment - i.e last output or last
permanently archived and subsequent restarts

classes.name: payu.sync.SyncToRemoteArchive

methods.name: payu.sync.SyncToRemoteArchive.add_outputs_to_sync
documentation:
Add paths of outputs in archive to sync. The last output is
protected

methods.name: payu.sync.SyncToRemoteArchive.add_restarts_to_sync
documentation:
Add paths and protected paths of restarts in archive to sync.
Last permanently-archived restart and subsequent restarts are
protected (as local date-based restart pruning uses the last-saved
restart as a checkpoint for a datetime)

methods.name: payu.sync.SyncToRemoteArchive.add_extra_source_paths
documentation: Add additional paths to sync to remote archive

methods.name: payu.sync.SyncToRemoteArchive.set_excludes_flags
documentation: Add lists of patterns of filepaths to exclude from sync commands

methods.name: payu.sync.SyncToRemoteArchive.build_cmd
documentation: Given a source path to sync, return a rsync command

methods.name: payu.sync.SyncToRemoteArchive.run_cmd
documentation: Given an source path, build and run rsync command

methods.name: payu.sync.SyncToRemoteArchive.git_runlog
documentation: Add git runlog to remote archive

methods.name: payu.sync.SyncToRemoteArchive.run
documentation: Build and run rsync cmds to remote remote archive
Class used for archiving experiment outputs to a remote directory

functions.name: payu.fsops.required_libs
documentation:
Runs ldd command and parses the output.
This function should only be called once per binary
i.e. Use a singleton pattern in the caller object.
PARAMETERS:
    string bin_path: full path to the binary
RETURN:
    dict: {filename-of-lib: fullpath-of-file}

functions.name: payu.fsops.list_archive_dirs
documentation: Return a sorted list of restart or output directories in archive

functions.name: payu.fsops.run_script_command
documentation:
Run a user defined script or command.

Parameters
----------
script_cmd : string
    String of user-script command defined in configuration file
control_path : Path
    The control directory of the experiment

Raises
------
RuntimeError
    If there's was an error running the user-script

functions.name: payu.fsops.needs_subprocess_shell
documentation:
Check if command contains shell specific values. For example, file
redirections, pipes or logical operators.

Parameters
----------
command: string
    String of command to run in subprocess call

Returns
-------
bool
    Returns True if command requires a subprocess shell, False otherwise

documentation:
Helper recursive function to attempt running a script command.

----------

control_path: Path
    The control directory to use for resolving relative filepaths, if file
    is not found

methods.name: payu.fsops.DuplicateKeyWarnLoader.construct_mapping
documentation:
Add warning for duplicate keys in yaml file, as currently
PyYAML overwrites duplicate keys even though in YAML, keys
are meant to be unique

Simple wrappers around git commands

Using the GitPython library for interacting with Git

Otherwise, if catch_error is true, it will return None
